name: go_build
description: Build a Go project with optional CGO_ENABLED=0 cross-compilation. Runs 'go build' with the specified package and output path, then reports binary info (file type, size, version). Replaces 3-4 separate commands: cd <repo>, go build, file <binary>, ls -la <binary>.
when_to_use: Use when you need to build a Go project to test compilation, create a binary for testing, or verify the project compiles correctly. Replaces the common pattern of 'cd <project> && go build -o <output> <pkg> && file <output> && ls -la <output>'. Automatically enables CGO_ENABLED=0 for cross-platform builds.

parameters.name: project_root
parameters.description: Root directory of the Go project (e.g. /app)

parameters.name: build_pkg
parameters.description: Package to build, relative to project_root (default: '.', i.e. the main module)

parameters.name: cgo_disabled
parameters.description: Set CGO_ENABLED=0 for static/cross compilation (default: 'true'). Use 'false' if CGO is needed.

examples.expected: Build /app/cmd/ to /tmp/k6 with CGO disabled, show binary info.
Build root package with CGO enabled, show binary info.

cost_saving_rationale: Combines 3-4 separate commands (cd, go build, file, ls, optional version check) into a single script call. The CGO_ENABLED=0 flag avoids a common failure pattern where agents forget to set it, saving an extra round trip. The automatic binary verification (file + ls + version) eliminates additional verification steps.
